name: Chand Baori
description: Thousands of exquisitely carved stone water storage wells, hundreds of stone steps fill this exquisite medieval stepwell.

name: Hawa Mahal (Palace of Winds)
description: The 953 windows undoubtedly make this the world's most beautiful screened porch.

name: The Root Bridges of Cherrapunji
description: Centuries-old bridges grown from tangled roots.

name: Skeletons of Roopkund Lake
description: A lake with hundreds of ancient skeletons surrounding it. The surprise is what killed them.

name: Jal Mahal
description: More than half of this Indian palace is drowned but it looks all the more spectacular for it.

name: The Blue City of Jodhpur
description: Once an indicator of social class, the color blue has come to define this city on the edge of the Thar Desert.

name: Ellora Caves

name: Mahabat Maqbara
description: This otherworldly palace-mausoleum complex is the stuff eternal dreams are made of.

name: Shatrunjaya Hill
description: A mecca for India's Jains.

name: The Great Wall of India
description: The second-longest continuous wall on the planet protects a hidden secret in the deserts of India.

name: Hanuman’s Temple
description: Sacred Indian temple that’s all monkey business.

name: Hampi
description: The remains of what was, not so long ago, the world's largest city.

name: Nek Chand Saini’s Rock Garden
description: A palatial sculpture park was built illegally and in secret by a self-taught artist over 36 years.

name: Jantar Mantar
description: The world's second-largest sundial, made entirely out of stone.

name: Ajanta Caves
description: Rock-cut cave complex filled with ancient Buddhist artwork.

name: Great Banyan Tree
description: A single tree's 2,800 aerial roots give the appearance of a complete forest.

name: Golden Temple
description: This peaceful Sikh gurudwara serves around 50,000 free meals every day within its gilded walls.

name: Agrasen ki Baoli
description: A startling, centuries-old subterranean marvel hides in plain sight.

name: Bahá'í Lotus Temple
description: A blooming house of worship for the masses.

name: India's Giant Technicolor Squirrels
description: These particularly resplendent rodents are the state animal of Maharashtra.

name: Elephanta Caves
description: An extraordinary "City of Caves" carved from solid stone.

name: Rani Ki Vav

name: Meenakshi Amman Temple
description: This ancient Hindu temple is covered in thousands of colorful statues and has a long mythological history.

name: Bhangarh Fort
description: This abandoned fort is said to be the most haunted place in India, so much so that entry is prohibited after sunset.

name: Hill Forts of Rajasthan
description: A complex of six forts embedded in the Rajasthan hills.

name: Mahila Baag Jhalra
description: Hidden in the shadow of a rocky fortress is one concubine’s long-forgotten legacy.

name: Key Monastery
description: This hilltop warren of monastic dwellings is the result of centuries worth of Mongol attacks.

name: Chittorgarh Fort
description: A former bastion of Rajput chivalry, this massive hill fort is the site of legend and tragedy.

name: Mysore Palace
description: Modest by day, illuminated majestically at sunset.

name: Cafe Lota
description: Nestled among the artifacts of the National Crafts Museum, this hidden gem offers hard-to-find regional foods from all over India.

name: Auroville
description: This experimental utopian "city of the future" in rural India may hold a dark secret.

name: Temples of Khajuraho

name: Mehrangarh Fort

name: Kalavantin Durg
description: A centuries-old lookout post still sits atop a natural stone spire.

name: Mayong - The Land of Black Magic
description: The Indian capital of black magic and witchcraft.

name: South Park Street Cemetery
description: An eclectic mix of influences rot together in harmony in this India necropolis.

name: Jaisalmer Fort

name: Sunderwala Burj
description: This beautiful 16th-century necropolis marks an astonishing feat in heritage conservation.

name: Stepped Tanks of Hampi
description: The ancient water tanks remain some of the ruined city’s most beautiful architecture.

name: College Street
description: The world's largest secondhand book market is a haven for bibliophiles and bargain hunters.

name: Nagarjunsagar-Srisailam Tiger Reserve
description: The largest tiger reserve in India is a vast expanse dotted with ancient ruins of Nagarjuna Viswa Vidyalayam.

name: Nahoum and Sons

name: Modhera Sun Temple
description: An architectural marvel that dates back to the zenith of Indian architecture.

name: Khari Baoli Rooftop
description: The roof of one of the world’s largest spice markets provides a grand view over the neighborhood's glorious bustle.

name: Murud Janjira fort
description: A nearly unconquerable island-fort in the Indian Ocean.

name: Adalaj Ni Vav
description: A deeply beautiful 15th-century stepwell with a tragic story.

name: Karni Mata Temple
description: Deep in the Thar desert, a temple crawling with thousands of holy rats.

name: Kolukkumalai Tea Estate

name: Ranthambore Fort

name: Ruins of Mandu
description: A centuries-old fortress city lies in ruin.

name: Mahakali Caves
description: Nineteen ancient rock-cut Buddhist caves can be found on the outskirts of Mumbai.

name: Gol Gumbaz
description: This 17th-century mausoleum is topped with an acoustic marvel.

name: Neemrana Baori

name: Fatehpur Sikri

name: Mahabodhi Temple
description: Siddhartha Gautama sat here and received enlightenment.

name: Gandhi Smriti
description: Time has stopped at the house where Gandhi died.

name: Iron Pillar of Delhi

name: Sheroes' Hangout

name: Bara Imambara
description: This magnificent 18th-century hall consists of a maze of nearly a thousand interconnected passages.

name: Sati Handprints
description: Fifteen Jodhpur queens left their handprints before going to their fiery death.

name: Netaji Shubash Chandra Bose Island
description: An abandoned settlement originally established by British colonialists in the Andaman archipelago.

name: Sulabh Museum of Toilets

name: Masrur Temple
description: 15 monolithic temples emulate an eroding sand castle.

name: Guru Sweet Mart

name: Kasol: Little Israel of the Himalayas

description: India's first open-air public art district sits in the heart of the country's capital.

description: This weekly bazaar started in the 1960s as a way for hippie travelers to barter their goods.

name: Cenotaphs of Orchha

name: Bull Surfing in Kerala

name: Chausath Yogini Temple
description: This ancient goddess temple inspired the design of India’s Parliament House.

name: Bibi Ka Maqbara
description: Half-size replica of the Taj Mahal.

name: Amber Fort
description: This opulent 16th-century Maharaja’s palace is a unique mix of Hindu and Muslim designs.

name: Neuropathology Brain Museum
description: India’s only Brain Museum displays over 200 fascinating ways that your brain might kill you.

name: Pushkar Brahma Temple
description: There are millions of temples in India, but only one prominent temple dedicated to the Hindu creator god.

name: Ratneshwar Mahadev Temple
description: Known as the leaning temple, the sacred structure has somehow developed a severe nine-degree slant.

name: The Adiyogi Shiva Statue

name: Thimmamma Marrimanu
description: The world's largest banyan tree is followed by a community of worshippers.

name: Jagannath Temple

name: The Magen David Synagogue

name: Gaitore Ki Chhatriyan
description: Ornate, dome-topped cenotaphs fill this royal crematorium.

name: Cremation Ghats of Varanasi
description: Many Hindus make the long pilgrimage to this holy city to have their remains burned on the banks of the Ganges River.

name: Antilia
description: World's most expensive home.

name: Lonar Lake
description: This rare basalt crater lake is used to study the Moon and Mars.

name: Sidi Saiyyed Mosque
description: This 16th-century mosque is the hallmark of the African architectural legacy in India.

name: Monsoon Palace
description: This magical place built as an astronomical center starred as a James Bond villain's lair.

name: Bylakuppe
description: The world's largest Tibetan refugee settlement.

name: Arq-e-Gulab
description: An old curiosity shop holds on to a centuries-old tradition of manually-distilled rose water.

name: Gilbert Hill
description: A 66-million-year-old rock formation stands tall over Mumbai, offering stellar city views.

name: Gilbert Hill
description: A 66-million-year-old rock formation stands tall over Mumbai, offering stellar city views.

name: Tiger Cave
description: Picnic on the seashore in the footsteps of ancient kings, protected by giant mythical cat beasts.

name: Terreti Bazaar

name: Marble Palace
description: This lavish estate recalls the opulent history of Calcutta.

name: Alamparai Fort

name: Tashiding Monastery

name: Hanging Pillar of Lepakshi
description: Inside a beautiful Indian temple is a seemingly magical floating pillar that doesn't touch the ground.

name: Ajam Emba
description: This women-run eatery and cooking school is putting India's long-neglected tribal cuisines back on the menu.

name: Marwari Horses
description: Old and brave breed of warhorses known for their elegant, curved ears.

name: Narlai Stepwell
description: Designed to serve primarily the Maharaja, this is an interesting variation on the traditional stepped well.

name: The Apatani Tribal Village of Ziro
description: Hidden in the rain-forests of Arunachal Pradesh in Northeast India lies the village of Ziro, home to the Apatanis.

name: The Apatani Tribal Village of Ziro
description: Hidden in the rain-forests of Arunachal Pradesh in Northeast India lies the village of Ziro, home to the Apatanis.

name: Narlai Stepwell
description: Designed to serve primarily the Maharaja, this is an interesting variation on the traditional stepped well.

name: Rajaram Meghwal Burial Place
description: Legend has it a commoner volunteered to be buried alive in the foundation so that the great fortress could be built.

description: One of the tallest mountains in India was closed for years after a botched CIA mission.

name: Jwala Ji Temple of Kangra
description: Eternal flame said to be a Hindu Goddesses fiery tounge.

name: The Triveni Sangam

name: Maqtha Art District

name: Bhimbetka Rock Shelters

name: Gomateshvara

name: Kwan Kung Temple

name: John Hessing's Tomb

name: Lucky Restaurant

name: Jama Masjid

name: Thiruvalluvar Statue

description: One man single-handedly dug a road right through a mountain after his wife died due to its interference.

name: Karpur Baoli

name: Cherrapunji

name: Unlock Hundarman Museum of Memories
description: A grassroots collection showcasing personal objects of family members separated by the post-1971 border between India and Pakistan.

name: Thiba Palace
description: This palace was built for the last king of Myanmar.

name: Cheraman Juma Masjid
description: Inside India's oldest mosque, a lamp is said to have burnt for 1,000 years without going out.

name: Ohel David Synagogue
description: Said to be the largest synagogue built in India.

name: Statues of Medici Lions
description: Four Medici Lions guard a heritage old stone bridge that was constructed in 1867.

name: Café Goodluck
description: This local institution has been serving Parsi staples since 1935.

name: The Old Water Tower
description: More than a century old, this heritage water tower is located in the middle of the high-rises of Pune.

name: The Arch of Conception
description: One of the few surviving gates into Old Goa, the former capital of Portuguese India.

name: Har Ki Pauri
description: The Hindu god Vishnu is said to have walked at this Hindu pilgrimage site.

name: Sapindya Mahadev Temple
description: An ancient temple of Lord Shiva was rediscovered in an excavation in 2003.

name: Shree Ganesh Darshan Museum
description: Located within a temple complex, this museum has a fantastic collection of around 500 idols of Lord Ganesh.

name: Ghanta Ghar
description: The clock tower is a prominent landmark in the bustling commercial hub in the heart of Jodhpur, showcasing intricate architecture and cultural significance.

name: Reis Magos Fort
description: A nearly impregnable 16th-century fort is one of Goa's oldest.

name: Statue of Jhalkaribai
description: Dedicated to the life of a brave woman soldier.

name: Nana Wada
description: A combination of Peshwa and British Gothic Revival architecture.

name: Nagarkar Wada
description: A 19th-century mansion with a Gothic entrance in the center of Pune.

name: Ima Market
description: Established in the 16th century, this "mother's market" is run entirely by women.

name: Losel Doll Museum
description: The art and tradition of Tibetan costumes are preserved through these dolls made by Buddhist monks.

name: Houseboats of Dal Lake
description: Hundreds of luxurious boats line this gorgeous lake surrounded by mountains.

name: Memorial Stupa of Helena Roerich
description: Outside a hilltop monastery in India, a monument to a 20th-century Russian mystic.

name: Church of Our Lady of the Rosary
description: Considered one of the oldest surviving churches in Goa.

name: Crypt at Se Cathedral
description: A mysterious burial crypt that was rediscovered inside a cathedral in Velha Goa.

name: Sevasi Vav
description: This 500-year-old stepwell descends seven levels deep into the ground.

name: Army & Navy Building
description: This 19th-century heritage building once housed a department store that supplied British provisions in the city.

name: Anandashram Sanstha
description: A historic organization dedicated towards the preservation of Sanskrit texts.

name: Hissar Fort Stairs
description: One of the last few remnants of an ancient fort which once stood in the oldest area of Pune.

name: World War I Memorial
description: An obelisk designed in memory of the soldiers from the city and district who fought in The Great War.

name: Alai Minar
description: On the grounds of the famous Qutab Minar lies another unfinished tower meant to be twice as tall.

name: Underground Tunnel at Mastani Lake
description: Once a passageway that led to the Shaniwar Wada palace fort.

name: Kala Ghoda Statue
description: A tribute to the area's namesake and King Edward VII.

name: Bhausaheb Rangari Bhavan Museum
description: A 19th-century house filled with secret trap doors and hidden basements has been turned into a museum.

name: Museum of Cartoon Art
description: On the university grounds, this unique museum is dedicated to cartoons and their importance in society.

name: Shree Nageshwar Shiva Temple
description: This 700-year-old temple is one of the city's oldest.

name: Heritage Charkha Museum
description: A tiny museum dedicated to the charkha, a traditional spinning wheel that became a symbol of national resistance during Gandhi's freedom movements.

name: Parvati Hill Chapha Tree
description: Said to be the oldest tree in Pune.

name: Twelve Pound Howitzer
description: In the courtyard of a popular museum stands a piece of artillery from 1859.

name: Kraken Habitat
description: A unique zoo of exotic animals with a cafe and a co-working space.

name: Flora Fountain
description: An iconic fountain dedicated to the Roman goddess Flora stands in the heart of Mumbai.

name: The Statue of Bajirao Peshwa
description: Dedicated to one of the greatest military minds of the Maratha Empire.

name: Narayan Darwaja
description: One of the five gates into the fortress of to Shaniwar Wada was renamed to commemorate the 10th Peshwa of the Maratha Empire.

name: Pune Nagar Vachan Mandir
description: The oldest library in Pune.

name: Blades of Glory Cricket Museum
description: Believed to be the largest cricket museum in the world.

name: Fort Aguada Jail Museum
description: A former prison within a seaside fort has now been turned into an interactive museum.

name: Old Goa Cemetery
description: This cemetery in Velha Goa was once the site of several historical religious buildings.

name: Museum of Christian Art
description: Located within a 17th-century convent, this museum features centuries-old Christian art pieces that meld European and Asian influences.

name: Port Trust War Memorial
description: A sandstone lamp post in Mumbai's business district honors the war efforts of the Port Trust and its employees who fell during World War I.

name: University Tunnel
description: This 300-foot-long underground tunnel was once used to transport food from the kitchen into the main building.

name: Amruteshwar Siddheshwar Temple Complex
description: A cluster of 18th-century temples with an underground shrine.

name: The Memorial of Nanasaheb Peshwa
description: A tribute to the man who helped develop Pune.

name: Kirkee War Cemetery
description: This neatly maintained cemetery honors the sacrifice of the brave soldiers who laid down their lives during World War I and World War II.

name: Blossom Book House
description: One of India’s largest second-hand bookstores, with more than 600,000 volumes lining its walls.

name: Maratha War Memorial
description: A stone monument dedicated to Indian soldiers who fought and died for the British Indian Army during World War I.

name: Eran Pillar of Goparaja
description: India's oldest sati stone, attributed to an otherwise unknown king of the ancient Gupta Empire.

name: Taljai Mansion
description: The crumbling ruins of an old stone mansion sit atop a hill in the middle of Pune.

name: Pune’s First Lift
description: The vintage, artistic cage of the city’s first lift welcomes visitors to an old heritage building.

name: The Modak and The Mount Street Lamps
description: Artistic streetlamps depicting Lord Ganesh’s favourite food and divine mount around an iconic temple of the deity in the heart of Pune.

name: Empress Botanical Garden
description: This sprawling oasis of green in the middle of the city has a vast collection of beautiful flora.

name: Shaniwar Wada
description: This 18th century Marathi fort is adorned with a spiked front gate meant to repel enemy elephants.

name: Tulshibaug Ram Temple
description: The 18th-century temple was once the tallest structure in Pune, and surrounded by a garden of tulsi.

name: Zero Stone of Pune
description: One in a series of historical markers from the massive 19th-century undertaking to map all of India.

name: Gate of St. Paul's College
description: All that remains of a 16th-century Jesuit school is this carved, worn gate.

name: Charaideo Moidam
description: The 700-year old burial mounds of the Ahom Kingdom.

name: Emblems of the Erstwhile Governors
description: These stone carvings represent the various governors that once ruled Mumbai.

name: Fort Aguada Lighthouse
description: A modern lighthouse near Fort Aguada offers stunning views of the surrounding peninsula.

name: Fish Building
description: The fin-tastically detailed headquarters of the National Fisheries Development Board.

name: The Old Gate of Vaibhaveshwar Navagraha Temple
description: On the Mutha riverbank near the center of Pune stands a centuries-old temple with a fascinating old stone entrance.

name: Cannon of Banastarim
description: This 16th-century cannon is thought to be the oldest piece of forge-welded wrought iron in India.

name: Dharohar: National Museum of Customs & GST
description: A collection of items seized by the Indian Customs have been kept for display at the Old Customs House in Panaji.

name: Zapurza Museum of Art & Culture
description: A huge collection of artifacts in a picturesque setting.

name: Galteshwar Mahadev Temple
description: A 12-century Shiva temple nestled along a picturesque riverbank.

name: Lal Mahal
description: A historic palace in the center of Pune where the founder of the Maratha Empire spent his formative years.

name: Church and Convent of St. Francis of Assisi
description: This church in Old Goa was established by eight Portuguese Franciscan friars who landed there in 1517.

name: Maruti Temple
description: An iconic temple dedicated to Lord Hanuman resides atop a hill in the heart of Panaji.

name: The Nizam's Guest House
description: This stately building once housed the office of the university for a brief period of time.

name: Parvati Nandan Ganpati Temple
description: This 17th-century temple gave the area of Ganeshkhind its name.

name: Keibul Lamjao
description: This is the world's only floating national park, and the last natural home of the majestic sangai deer.

name: Manjarabad Fort
description: This is no regular fort! It’s a star.

name: Convent of the Discalced Carmelites
description: The ruins of a 17th-century Carmelite Convent atop a hill in Old Goa.

name: Panaji's Heritage Steps
description: These colorful staircases can be found around Goa’s capital.

name: Jayantrao Tilak Rose Garden
description: Unassuming public garden with hundreds of rose varieties.

name: Dona Paula's Tombstone
description: Legends of adultery, love affairs, and charity swirl around this mysterious 17th-century Portuguese woman.

name: The Chapel of St. Catherine
description: This chapel was built by Afonso de Albuquerque in 1510 to commemorate his entry into the city of Goa on St. Catherine’s Day.

name: Collection of Old Engraved Stones
description: An open-air collection of historical stones with engravings and inscriptions in Old Goa.

name: Servants of India Society Monument
description: A hilltop stone monument commemorating the establishment of a historic social welfare organization.

name: Sambhar Lake
description: India’s largest inland salt lake, whose blinding white salt flats create one of the most surreal landscapes in the Thar Desert.

name: Pune Prarthana Samaj
description: This beautiful stone building houses a historic organization focused on social reforms.

name: Bharat Itihas Sanshodhak Mandal
description: A heritage stone building that preserves over a million historical documents and rare artifacts.

name: Tomb of Ishtur Phakda
description: The final resting place of a British officer killed during the First Anglo-Maratha War.

name: Tambat Ali
description: Home of coppersmiths whose families have been working here since the 17th century.

name: Tomb of Lord Cornwallis
description: The man who surrendered his army to George Washington is buried halfway across the world.

name: Harihareshwar Rock Formations
description: Fascinating rock formations on the cliffs of a sacred coastal town.

name: Sinquerim Beach Bastion
description: An old bastion at the base of Aguada Fort protrudes into the sea, offering picturesque views.

name: The Convent of St. Dominic's Cross
description: The only surviving relic of the abandoned Dominican convent.

name: Mujumdar Wada
description: This 18th-century building is said to be one of the oldest heritage mansions in Pune.

name: Shivsrushti
description: A historical theme park based on the life and times of Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj.

name: Vruddheshwar Cave Temple
description: Cut into the rock face of a hill, this ancient cave temple is believed to be one of the oldest structures in Pune.

name: Someshwar Temple
description: An ancient Lord Shiva temple contains replica models of all twelve Jyotirlinga temples.

name: The Letterbox With a Crown
description: In the premises of Pune Head Post Office stands a curious letterbox topped with the design of a crown.

name: The Old Archiepiscopal Palace
description: In Old Goa, the former residence of the archbishop now houses a permanent exhibition.

name: Chapel of Our Lady of the Mount
description: This 16th-century hilltop chapel is one of the oldest churches in Old Goa.

name: University Stone Quarry Lake
description: Now a small lake, stone quarried from this spot was used to build the local university building.

name: Guna Caves
description: The forest hums softly, like it’s alive and listening, outside the “Devil’s Kitchen.”

name: The 'Old Secretariat'
description: Panaji’s oldest surviving building, which was once a palace and viceroy residence, now houses the Goa State Museum.

name: Sao Bras Church
description: This 16th century church is Goa's fascinating connection with Croatia.

name: Institute Menezes Braganza
description: The entrance hall of this old cultural institute is decorated with Azulejo artwork depicting Vasco da Gama’s journey to India.

name: Casa de Polvora Millstones
description: The millstones of an ancient Portuguese gunpowder factory have been kept on display at Old Goa.

name: Casa da Moeda
description: The erstwhile Portuguese Era mint house of Goa.

name: The Charging Bull and the Common Man
description: These sculptures represent the general public's hopes and aspirations of financial prosperity.

name: Aqsa Mosque
description: The founding place and chief symbol of the Ahmadiyya sect of Islam.

name: Albert Edward Institute Library
description: A library built to commemorate Edward VII's visit to Pune.

name: George Orwell Birthplace
description: The author of Animal Farm and 1984 was born to a civil servant in British India.

name: Levitating Stone of Shivapur
description: According to legend, a Sufi saint decreed that 11 men can lift this roughy 150-pound stone with only their index fingers upon invoking his name.

name: Maratha Empire Murals
description: These relief murals on Parvati Hill depict significant milestones of the Maratha Empire.

name: Tanpura Bridge
description: This recently constructed pedestrian bridge has been shaped like a classical Indian musical instrument to reflect the city’s cultural ethos.

name: Ponte de Linhares Causeway
description: Believed to have been the longest bridge in the world when it was completed in 1634.

name: Mandovi Memorial Cross
description: Dedicated to the victims of a tragic ferry disaster in Goa.

name: Baloba Munja Mandir
description: This temple, in the oldest area of Pune, may date back to the second century.

name: Ribandar Riverside Viewpoint Cross
description: This cross marks the site of a 16th-century Portuguese gunpowder factory.

name: Statue of Abbe Faria
description: In the heart of Panaji, a monument to the pioneer of modern hypnotism.

name: Portuguese Bell at Menavali Ghat
description: Taken as a war trophy in 1739, this Portuguese bell now hangs in an ancient Hindu temple.

name: Vikram Pendse Cycle Museum
description: In a Pune bungalow, a quirky museum showcases over 150 vintage bicycles.

name: Anchor Point Stones
description: Historic anchors for the animals that once carried India’s mail.

name: Cross of the Church of Our Lady of Light
description: This memorial marks a long-gone church in Old Goa.

name: Perarignar Anna Memorial
description: The largest funeral ever held was for one of Tamil's most influential politicians.

name: Gaspar Dias Fort Cannon
description: This cannon was part of a 16th-century fort near Miramar Beach in Panaji.

name: Replicas of Jyotirlinga Temples
description: Twelve model temples sit inside this 900-year old Hindu complex.

name: Goa's Daugim Cross
description: This memorial cross along a roadside in Old Goa, India marks the spot of once bustling, but long-gone community.

name: Vesavar Art Gallery
description: A contemporary art space located within a charming hundred year old heritage building.

name: Chapora Fort
description: Once an important military landmark, this centuries old fort regained its popularity in modern times due to an iconic Bollywood film.

name: Bann Ki Ghati Watch Base
description: A ruined hilltop watch base in the Aravalli ridge above Jaipur, where broken fort walls and a forgotten lookout overlook Man Sagar Lake, reachable only by scrambling through cactus-covered desert trails.
